title: Annona neoecuadorensis
intro: Annona neoecuadorensis, also known as the Ecuadorian custard apple, is a species of flowering plant in the Annonaceae family, native to the tropical regions of South America. The fruit of the Annona neoecuadorensis plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Annona neoecuadorensis include annonaceous acetogenins, which are known to exhibit cytotoxic and anti-cancer activities, as well as other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Annona neoecuadorensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
taxonomy: Annona neoecuadorensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Annona, which consists of tropical fruits such as custard apples. This plant is native to the tropical regions of the Americas and is known for its edible fruit. The classification of Annona neoecuadorensis places it within the order Magnoliales and class Magnoliopsida. Further classification of Annona neoecuadorensis includes the family Annonaceae, genus Annona, and species A. neoecuadorensis.
